name: Nova Scotia (Canada)

colors.symbolism: the field, recalling the colours of Saint Andrew's Cross
the gold field of the inescutcheon
the royal lion rampant of Scotland and its double tressure

description: A blue saltire on a white field (the Scottish Saint Andrew's cross reversed) with an inescutcheon of the Royal Arms of Scotland (a red lion rampant within a double tressure on gold) at the centre.
history: The flag uses the shield of the provincial coat of arms, which was granted by King Charles I in 1625, making it the oldest provincial flag in Canada and the oldest British colonial flag still in use. Its use as a flag was authorised in 1858 by the General Assembly of Nova Scotia and confirmed by royal warrant in 2013.

features.symbolism: A blue saltire on a white field, the colours of the Scottish Saint Andrew's Cross reversed, recalling the province's name (Latin for 'New Scotland').

features.role: inescutcheon of the Royal Arms of Scotland (a red lion rampant within a double tressure flory-counter-flory on gold)
features.symbolism: The royal arms of Scotland, granted to Nova Scotia in its 1625 charter as the only Canadian province to bear arms older than Confederation.
